title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install all required packages for DU-VPT experiments including PyTorch, timm, and other dependencies

steps.step1: Activate virtual environment: source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA: pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install timm for Vision Transformers: pip install timm>=0.9.0
steps.step4: Install scientific computing packages: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn
steps.step5: Install utility packages: pip install tqdm pillow pyyaml tensorboard
steps.step6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step7: Create experiment directory structure: mkdir -p experiments/{baselines,ablations,analysis} results/{checkpoints,logs,figures} data

title: Download and Prepare Datasets
description: Download ImageNet-C, ImageNet-R, and ImageNet-Sketch datasets. Create calibration set from ImageNet validation.

steps.step1: Download ImageNet-C from https://github.com/hendrycks/robustness (15 corruption types, severity 1-5)
steps.step2: Download ImageNet-R from https://github.com/hendrycks/imagenet-r (30,000 images, 200 classes)
steps.step3: Download ImageNet-Sketch from https://github.com/HaohanWang/ImageNet-Sketch (50,000 images)
steps.step4: Create calibration set: Sample 1000 images from ImageNet validation set (different from test)
steps.step5: Compute and cache dataset statistics: mean, std, number of samples per dataset
steps.step6: Verify dataset integrity: Check all images loadable, correct number of samples
steps.step7: Preprocess: Resize to 224x224, normalize with ImageNet statistics (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225])

title: Compute Calibration Statistics for Uncertainty Decomposition
description: Pre-compute layer-wise statistics from calibration set for epistemic uncertainty estimation

steps.step1: Load pre-trained ViT-B/16 from timm (vit_base_patch16_224.augreg_in21k_ft_in1k)
steps.step2: Run calibration set through model, extract features at each of 12 layers
steps.step3: Compute mean and variance statistics for each layer: mu_cal[l], sigma_cal[l] for l in 1..12
steps.step4: Compute Fisher Information matrix for each layer on calibration set (for regularization)
steps.step5: Cache statistics to disk: experiments/calibration_stats_vitb16.pt
steps.step6: Verify statistics shape: [12, D] for means where D=768 (hidden dim)
steps.step7: Document calibration set composition: class distribution, sample IDs

title: Source Model Evaluation (No Adaptation)
description: Evaluate pre-trained ViT-B/16 without any adaptation as the lower bound baseline

steps.step1: Load ViT-B/16 model: model = timm.create_model('vit_base_patch16_224.augreg_in21k_ft_in1k', pretrained=True)
steps.step2: Evaluate on ImageNet-C (all 15 corruptions, severity 5): Compute top-1 accuracy, mCE, ECE
steps.step3: Evaluate on ImageNet-R: Compute top-1 accuracy, per-class accuracy, ECE
steps.step4: Evaluate on ImageNet-Sketch: Compute top-1 accuracy, ECE
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) for data loading order consistency
steps.step6: Save results: results/source_model_results.json with metrics per dataset
steps.step7: Estimated runtime: 15 minutes (no adaptation overhead)

title: Tent Baseline (BN-based TTA)
description: Implement and evaluate Tent entropy minimization adapted for ViT (updating LayerNorm statistics)

steps.step1: Implement Tent for ViT: Update LayerNorm statistics instead of BatchNorm
steps.step2: Use learning rate 1e-3 for statistics update, momentum 0.9
steps.step3: Evaluate on ImageNet-C (severity 5), ImageNet-R, ImageNet-Sketch
steps.step4: Measure: top-1 accuracy, mCE, ECE, adaptation time per sample
steps.step5: Run with 3 seeds (42, 123, 456), report mean ± std
steps.step6: Track parameter updates: Count LayerNorm parameters being modified
steps.step7: Save results: results/tent_results.json, estimated runtime: 15 minutes

title: EATA Baseline (Sample Selection + Fisher)
description: Implement EATA with sample selection based on entropy threshold and Fisher regularization

steps.step1: Implement EATA: Entropy threshold = 0.4*ln(num_classes), Fisher penalty weight = 2000
steps.step2: Filter samples with entropy < threshold for adaptation
steps.step3: Apply Fisher regularization to prevent forgetting
steps.step4: Evaluate on all three datasets with 3 seeds
steps.step5: Track: accuracy, sample selection ratio, forgetting score (source vs adapted gap)
steps.step6: Compare against Tent to verify implementation
steps.step7: Save results: results/eata_results.json, estimated runtime: 15 minutes

title: VPT-Deep Baseline (Uniform Prompts)
description: Implement VPT-Deep with prompts at all layers (no selection) for uniform prompt baseline

steps.step1: Implement VPT-Deep: Add 10 learnable prompt tokens to each of 12 layers
steps.step2: Prompt dimension: 10 x 768, initialized from N(0, 0.02)
steps.step3: Use entropy minimization loss for test-time adaptation
steps.step4: Learning rate: 5e-3, Adam optimizer, 1 step per sample
steps.step5: Evaluate on all datasets with 3 seeds
steps.step6: Track: accuracy, number of parameters updated (120 x 768 = 92,160 params)
steps.step7: Save results: results/vpt_deep_results.json, estimated runtime: 20 minutes

title: PALM Baseline (Layer Selection + Weight Updates)
description: Implement PALM-style layer selection using gradient magnitude and weight updates at selected layers

steps.step1: Implement PALM: Compute gradient magnitude (KL to uniform) at each layer
steps.step2: Select top-k layers (k=4) with highest gradient magnitude for update
steps.step3: Update MLP weights at selected layers: learning rate 1e-4
steps.step4: Evaluate on all datasets with 3 seeds
steps.step5: Track: accuracy, layer selection frequency, forgetting score, parameters updated
steps.step6: This is the STRONG baseline we compare against
steps.step7: Save results: results/palm_results.json, estimated runtime: 30 minutes

title: DU-VPT Main Method (Full Implementation)
description: Implement and evaluate full DU-VPT with uncertainty decomposition, shift diagnosis, and targeted prompts

steps.step1: Implement uncertainty decomposition:
steps.step1a: - Aleatoric: Local feature consistency using spatial neighbors (4-connected)
steps.step1b: - Epistemic: Feature deviation from calibration statistics
steps.step2: Implement shift diagnosis:
steps.step3: Implement targeted prompts:
steps.step3a: - Structure prompts (local smoothness loss) for low-level shifts
steps.step3b: - Semantic prompts (entropy minimization) for domain shifts
steps.step4: Hyperparameters: M=10 prompts/layer, lr=5e-3, Fisher penalty=2000
steps.step5: Evaluate on ImageNet-C, ImageNet-R, ImageNet-Sketch with 3 seeds
steps.step6: Track: accuracy, mCE, ECE, adaptation efficiency (layers adapted), diagnosis accuracy
steps.step7: Save results: results/duvpt_main_results.json, estimated runtime: 30 minutes

title: Ablation 1: Prompts vs Weight Updates at Selected Layers (CRITICAL)
description: Directly compares prompts vs weight updates at uncertain layers to isolate architectural contribution

steps.step1: Configuration A (DU-VPT): Prompts at uncertain layers, backbone frozen
steps.step2: Configuration B (PALM-style): Weight updates at uncertain layers (same selection strategy)
steps.step3: Configuration C (Uniform): Weight updates at ALL layers
steps.step4: Use same uncertainty-based layer selection for A and B for fair comparison
steps.step5: Evaluate on ImageNet-C (severity 5) and ImageNet-R with 3 seeds
steps.step6: Measure: accuracy, forgetting score, parameters updated, inference time
steps.step7: CRITICAL: This directly addresses the feedback about isolating the architectural contribution
steps.step8: Save results: results/ablation_prompt_vs_weight.json, estimated runtime: 25 minutes

title: Ablation 2: Selective vs Uniform Prompt Application
description: Compare uncertainty-guided layer selection vs random selection and uniform application

steps.step1: Configuration A: DU-VPT selective prompts at uncertain layers only
steps.step2: Configuration B: Uniform prompts at ALL 12 layers (VPT-Deep style)
steps.step3: Configuration C: Random layer selection (same number of layers as DU-VPT)
steps.step4: Configuration D: Fixed early layers only (1-4)
steps.step5: Configuration E: Fixed deep layers only (9-12)
steps.step6: Use same prompt type (semantic) for all configurations for fair comparison
steps.step7: Evaluate on ImageNet-C and ImageNet-R with 3 seeds
steps.step8: Track: accuracy, compute overhead, layer selection patterns

title: Ablation 3: Uncertainty Decomposition vs Single Metric
description: Validate that decomposed uncertainty (aleatoric+epistemic) outperforms single uncertainty metrics

steps.step1: Configuration A: DU-VPT full (decomposed uncertainty)
steps.step2: Configuration B: Single entropy metric only (TPT-style)
steps.step3: Configuration C: Single gradient magnitude (PALM-style)
steps.step4: Configuration D: Random uncertainty values
steps.step5: For B and C, use same layer selection logic but with single metric
steps.step6: Evaluate shift diagnosis accuracy: Compare predicted vs actual shift type
steps.step7: Evaluate on ImageNet-C (known corruption types) and ImageNet-R with 3 seeds
steps.step8: Track: accuracy, shift diagnosis accuracy, layer selection patterns

title: Ablation 4: Prompt Type Matching
description: Validate that matching prompt types to shift types improves performance

steps.step1: Configuration A: Matched prompts (structure for corruption, semantic for domain)
steps.step2: Configuration B: Always use semantic prompts
steps.step3: Configuration C: Always use structure prompts
steps.step4: Configuration D: Hybrid prompts (learned mixing) for all
steps.step5: Configuration E: Random prompt type selection
steps.step6: Test on ImageNet-C (needs structure prompts) and ImageNet-R (needs semantic)
steps.step7: Measure accuracy for each configuration on each shift type
steps.step8: Verify: Structure prompts better for corruption, semantic for domain shift

title: Ablation 5: Hyperparameter Sensitivity
description: Test robustness to key hyperparameters: prompt length, learning rate, thresholds

steps.step1: Vary prompt length M: [5, 10, 20, 50] tokens per layer
steps.step2: Vary learning rate: [1e-3, 5e-3, 1e-2, 5e-2]
steps.step3: Vary aleatoric threshold tau_alpha: [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4]
steps.step4: Vary epistemic threshold tau_epsilon: [0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0]
steps.step5: Test on subset: ImageNet-C Gaussian noise and ImageNet-R only
steps.step6: Run single seed for efficiency, track accuracy vs hyperparameter
steps.step7: Identify robust operating range for each hyperparameter

title: Forgetting and Robustness Analysis
description: Measure catastrophic forgetting and adaptation stability over time

steps.step1: Forgetting metric: Test on source ImageNet after adaptation to shifted data
steps.step2: Sequential adaptation: Adapt to ImageNet-C → ImageNet-R → ImageNet-Sketch
steps.step3: Track accuracy on each dataset after each adaptation step
steps.step4: Compare DU-VPT vs PALM vs Tent forgetting rates
steps.step5: Measure prediction consistency: Variance in predictions across adaptation steps
steps.step6: Run with 3 seeds, report forgetting score = acc_source_initial - acc_source_final
steps.step7: Save results: results/forgetting_analysis.json, estimated runtime: 15 minutes

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform statistical tests to validate improvements are significant

steps.step1: Collect all results with 3 seeds for each method
steps.step2: Perform paired t-test between DU-VPT and each baseline
steps.step3: Compute p-values, effect sizes (Cohen's d) for accuracy differences
steps.step4: Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons
steps.step5: Verify: p < 0.05 for improvement over strong baseline (PALM)
steps.step6: Generate table: Method | Accuracy | Std | p-value vs DU-VPT
steps.step7: Save results: results/statistical_tests.json, estimated runtime: 5 minutes

title: Layer Selection Pattern Analysis
description: Analyze which layers are selected for different corruption types to provide interpretable insights

steps.step1: Run DU-VPT on each ImageNet-C corruption type individually
steps.step2: Record: selected layers, aleatoric values, epistemic values for each
steps.step3: Compute average layer selection frequency per corruption type
steps.step4: Corruption categories: Noise (gaussian, shot, impulse), Blur (defocus, motion, zoom), Weather (snow, frost, fog), Digital (pixelate, jpeg)
steps.step5: Visualize heatmap: Corruption type × Layer (color = selection frequency)
steps.step6: Analyze: Do noise corruptions select early layers? Do weather effects select deeper?
steps.step7: Save layer selection data: results/layer_selection_patterns.json
steps.step8: This addresses feedback: 'what unique insights layer selection patterns reveal'

title: Uncertainty-Error Correlation Analysis
description: Analyze correlation between decomposed uncertainty and actual prediction errors

steps.step1: For each test sample, record: aleatoric uncertainty, epistemic uncertainty, prediction correctness
steps.step2: Bin samples by uncertainty levels (low/medium/high) for each component
steps.step3: Compute error rate within each bin
steps.step4: Hypothesis: High aleatoric → errors on corrupted but clear semantics; High epistemic → errors on clear but OOD images
steps.step5: Compute correlation coefficients: Pearson and Spearman between uncertainty and error
steps.step6: Generate calibration plots: Expected vs observed error for each uncertainty type
steps.step7: Save results: results/uncertainty_error_correlation.json, estimated runtime: 10 minutes

title: Computational Efficiency Analysis
description: Compare inference time and computational overhead across methods

steps.step1: Measure inference time per sample (average over 1000 samples)
steps.step2: Methods to compare: Source, Tent, EATA, PALM, VPT-Deep, DU-VPT
steps.step3: Break down DU-VPT time: Forward pass, uncertainty decomposition, layer selection, prompt update
steps.step4: Measure FLOPs using fvcore or similar tool
steps.step5: Measure peak GPU memory usage per method
steps.step6: Compute throughput: Samples per second
steps.step7: Generate bar plot: Method vs Inference Time with breakdown
steps.step8: Save results: results/computational_efficiency.json, estimated runtime: 15 minutes

title: Generate Main Paper Figures
description: Create figures for the research paper showing key results

steps.step1: Figure 1: Method overview diagram (conceptual, created with draw.io or similar)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Accuracy comparison bar chart - Method vs Dataset with error bars (3 seeds)
steps.step3: Figure 3: Layer selection heatmap - Corruption type × Layer, color = selection frequency
steps.step4: Figure 4: Ablation results grouped bar chart - Configuration vs Accuracy
steps.step5: Figure 5: Uncertainty decomposition visualization - Sample images with uncertainty maps
steps.step6: Figure 6: Accuracy vs Severity curve for ImageNet-C (all methods)
steps.step7: Save all figures: results/figures/figure_{1-6}.{pdf,png}
steps.step8: Use consistent color scheme, readable fonts, publication quality

title: Generate Paper Tables
description: Create tables for the paper with formatted results

steps.step1: Table 1: Main results - Dataset × Method matrix with mean±std accuracy
steps.step2: Table 2: Ablation study results - Component × Accuracy
steps.step3: Table 3: Layer selection statistics per corruption type
steps.table4: Table 4: Computational efficiency comparison - Method × (Time, Memory, Params, Throughput)
steps.step5: Table 5: Shift diagnosis accuracy by corruption category
steps.step6: Format: LaTeX tables for easy inclusion in paper
steps.step7: Save: results/tables/table_{1-5}.tex and .csv
steps.step8: Include all results with 3 seeds as mean ± std

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify all success criteria from proposal are met or document if refuted

steps.step1: Criterion 1: >2% improvement over uniform prompt application
steps.step1a: Check: DU-VPT vs VPT-Deep on each dataset, verify mean improvement > 2%
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Within 1% accuracy of weight-update adaptation
steps.step2a: Check: DU-VPT vs PALM on each dataset, verify |acc_diff| < 1%
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Lower catastrophic forgetting than PALM (>5% gap)
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Accurate shift-type diagnosis (>80% accuracy)
steps.step4a: Check: Compare predicted shift type vs known corruption type, verify > 80%
steps.step5: Criterion 5: Interpretable layer selection patterns
steps.step5a: Check: Verify patterns show early layers for corruption, deep for domain shift
steps.step6: Generate verdict: CONFIRMED if all criteria met, PARTIAL if some, REFUTED if key claims fail
steps.step7: Save summary: results/success_criteria_verification.json
